item.title: Govt unlikely to implement pay hikes
item.description: The government is unlikely to implement the proposed pay hikes for public officials and employees in the current financial year ending in June as the ongoing war in the Gulf has left it in a tight spot....

item.title: Taiwan to ship oil from Saudi Red Sea ports
item.description: Taiwan will redirect ships to bring crude oil from Saudi Arabian Red Sea ports, the government said Monday, as it seeks to avoid the disruption in the Strait of Hormuz......

item.title: Govt forms committee to evaluate projects: Khasru
item.description: Under the directive of prime minister Tarique Rahman, a special committee has been formed to conduct a comprehensive evaluation of ongoing development projects, aiming to eliminate wasteful spending and ensure alignment with the national interest....

item.title: Artemis crew to set space distance record
item.description: Four Artemis II astronauts are taking a giant step for humankind Monday when they shoot deeper into space than anyone before and glimpse parts of the Moon never seen by the naked eye....

item.title: Artemis crew to set space distance record
item.description: Four Artemis II astronauts are taking a giant step for humankind Monday when they shoot deeper into space than anyone before and glimpse parts of the Moon never seen by the naked eye....

item.title: Most city pumps run dry
item.description: Most city pumps ran dry on Monday, failing to provide fuel oils, especially octane and petrol, to consumers, while the government says it has to be rational in making the supply. ...

item.title: S Korean co to make camping items at BEPZA EZ
item.description: South Korean company PH Creative (BD) Limited will manufacture camping items, garment accessories, bags and luggage at the BEPZA Economic Zone at Mirsharai of Chattogram, according to the Bangladesh Export Processing Zones Authority......Bangladesh travel guide

item.title: Cancellation of trade deal with US govt demanded
item.description: The Ganatantrik Jukta Front, a combine of sevenpoliticalparties, on Monday at a protest rally in Dhaka called on the government to take immediate steps to scrap the trade deal signed with United States during the interim government....

item.title: Samsung launches French-door refrigerator with AI
item.description: Samsung Bangladesh on Monday launched its first-ever French-door refrigerator with built-in AI capability, the RF59......

item.title: Justice for Sagar, Runi demanded
item.description: Journalist leaders on Monday demanded justice for the murders of journalist couple Sagar Sarwar and Meherun Runi, who were hacked to death in their rented apartment at Rajabazar in Dhaka on February 11, 2012....

item.title: AB Bank signs deal with iRONman Company
item.description: AB Bank PLC has recently signed a memorandum of understanding with iRONman Company Limited, said a press release......Politics

item.title: Janata Bank opens sub-branch at Rampal
item.description: Janata Bank PLC on April 2 opened a sub-branch at Rampal Power Plant in Bagerhat, said a press release......

item.title: Bank Asia achieves Tk 4,000cr net deposit growth in Q1
item.description: Bank Asia PLC has recorded a milestone by achieving a net deposit growth of Tk 4,000 crore during the January-March quarter of 2026, reflecting strong customer confidence and the Bank’s continued commitment to sustainable growth......

item.title: BKB inaugurates Saharbati Bazar sub-branch
item.description: Bangladesh Krishi Bank on Monday opened its Saharbati Bazar sub-branch under Gangni branch of Meherpur Region, said a press release......

item.title: Implementation of ILO Convention 190 demanded
item.description: The Nari Unnayan Shakti concluded its two-day workshop titled Implementing ILO Convention 190 to Prevent Violence and Sexual Harassment Against Women at Workplace: Building Skilled Advocates and Public Mobilisation at the NUS Training Centre......Geographic Reference

item.title: DIU, Paramount Group sign deal for 3.4MW solar project
item.description: Daffodil International University and Paramount Group signed a power purchase agreement to implement a 3.4MW solar power project to increase the use of renewable energy......

item.title: Foundation training for lecturers begins at CUET
item.description: Chittagong University of Engineering and Technology vice-chancellor Professor Mahmud Abdul Matin Bhuiyan said that teaching is an art and its quality depends mainly on the skills, attitudes and modern teaching methods of teachers applied in the classroom....

item.title: Shaping knowledge-based future
item.description: EDUCATION has always been the pillar of national development; nevertheless, in the twenty first century its function has metamorphosed from simply passing knowledge from teacher to student. In a fast-changing global environment where technological progress, scientific discovery and complex social problems play a significant role, education needs to be updated......

item.title: Sovereignty, silence, struggle for fair global order
item.description: AT THIS moment in history, the world is not simply experiencing isolated conflicts. It is undergoing a deeper transition — political, economic, and psychological. Across Asia, Africa, Latin America, and parts of the Middle East, there is a growing sense that the existing global order no longer reflects the lived reality of most of humanity......Politics

item.title: Democratic accountability vs administrative control
item.description: BANGLADESH’S governance system functions within an ongoing tension between democratic accountability and administrative control. On one hand, democratic accountability requires elected officials to be answerable to citizens, maintain transparency and promote participatory decision-making. On the other hand, administrative control emphasises bureaucratic......

item.title: Urgent path to elimination
item.description: IN THE opening months of 2026, Bangladesh has witnessed an alarming resurgence of measles. According to the Directorate General of Health Services, laboratory-confirmed cases reached 676 by late March — a dramatic 75-fold increase from just nine in the same period of 2025. Hospital admissions for suspected measles have surpassed 2,300 children, with over......

item.title: PFAS contamination in supply water alarming
item.description: ACCESS to safe water is a fundamental right, but the government fails to ensure it for people of Dhaka. Despite knowing of the presence of carcinogenic elements in the supply water for about five years, the Dhaka Water Supply and Sewerage Authority took no remedial action. The Environment and Social Development Organisation in collaboration with Brown University......

item.title: Govt must resume nationwide Vitamin A campaign
item.description: WHILE the ongoing measles outbreak has already exposed weaknesses and lapses in the immunisation programme, disruption in the Vitamin A supplementation campaign is believed to have left millions of children vulnerable to severe measles complications and other infectious diseases. The supplementation campaign had already faced disruption during the......

item.title: Govt’s bank loan target surpassed in 9 months
item.description: Government borrowing from banks has already exceeded its annual target in March, well before the fiscal year ends, signaling mounting fiscal strain and increasing pressure on the financial system......

item.title: BB assures expanding EDF to $5 billion
item.description: Bangladesh Bank governor Md Mostaqur Rahman has assured business leaders that the Export Development Fund (EDF), created from the country’s foreign exchange reserves, will be expanded in phases to $5 billion to support exporters facing dollar constraints......Human rights reports

item.title: Govt adopts zero-tolerance on all forms of violence: AZM Zahid
item.description: Social welfare and women and children affairs minister AZM Zahid Hossain at a programme in Dhaka on Monday said that the government would adopt a zero-tolerance stance on all forms of violence against women and children....

item.description: A former Jubo Dal leader on Monday filed a complaint of crimes against humanity, accusing ousted prime minister Sheikh Hasina, former DGFI chief retired Lieutenant General Sheikh Mamun Khaled and 20 former law enforcement officials over his alleged......

item.title: Russian strikes kill 3 in Ukraine
item.description: Russian strikes on the southern Ukrainian port city of Odesa killed three people, including a two-year-old child, and cut power to thousands of residents in the early hours of Monday, officials said....

item.title: 21 illegal structures evicted in DMP drive
item.description: The Dhaka Metropolitan Police drive to remove encroachers from the city’s footpaths and roads continued on Monday with evicting 21 shops and establishments inside Sayedabad Bus Terminal....

item.description: The Foreign Investors’ Chamber of Commerce and Industry on Sunday urged the government to build a transparent, predictable, and investor-friendly tax system in Bangladesh......

item.title: Gold price drops by Tk 2,158
item.description: The price of gold in Bangladesh has been reduced by Tk 2,158 per bhari, bringing the new rate for 22-carat gold to Tk 245,819, the Bangladesh Jewellers Association announced on Monday......

item.title: UK trade envoy in Dhaka seeking to boost trade, economy
item.description: UK trade envoy to Bangladesh Baroness Winterton of Doncaste is visiting Dhaka this week to reinforce and expand UK-Bangladesh trade and economic partnership, as confirmed by government officials......

item.title: Boeing VP eyes aircraft leasing deal
item.description: Boeing vice-president Paul Righi on Monday paid a courtesy call on civil aviation and tourism minister Afroza Khanam Rita and state minister M Rashiduzzaman Millat at Bangladesh Secretariat in the city......

item.title: Immigration operation runs manually
item.description: Immigration officials at Osmani International Airport, Sylhet have been carrying out their activities manually since a late night fire damaged the immigration server on Sunday, disrupting its automation process....

item.title: Jatiya Ganatantrik Party observes 46th founding anniversary
item.description: Jatiya Ganatantrik Party-Jagpa, a partner of the Jamaat-led alliance, observed its 46th founding anniversary on Monday with the vow to implement the verdict of the February 12 referendum....

item.title: Naogaon BNP leaders accuse MP of abducting party activists
item.description: Leaders and activists of the ruling Bangladesh Nationalist Party on Monday accused the party’s lawmaker from Naogaon-4 constituency (Manda), Ekramul Bari Tipu, of abducting and torturing party members at his private residence, which they......Human rights reports

item.title: BMU new VC urges all staff to fulfil responsibilities with diligence
item.description: Vice-chancellor of the Bangladesh Medical University, Professor Dr FM Siddiqui, on Monday urged all staff to fulfil their responsibilities with diligence....

item.title: 11 held while entering India illegally
item.description: Border Guard Bangladesh has detained 11 individuals, including an Indian national, at the Maheshpur border in Jhenaidah on Sunday night, for allegedly attempting to cross into India without valid documents....

item.title: Vast tracts of boro crops submerged in Habiganj due to rain
item.description: Vast tracts of boro paddy fields have been inundated due to heavy rainfall over the past few days in eight unions across three upazilas of Habiganj....

item.title: BADC discusses non-urea fertiliser management
item.description: Agriculture minister Mohammad Amin Ur Rashid on Monday said that the development of the country cannot be fruitful without agriculture....

item.title: Fuel card mandatory for farmers in Chuadanga
item.description: NEW guidelines have been introduced for the distribution of diesel and petrol to farmers for irrigation at Jibannagar upazila in Chuadanga, making it mandatory to present an agricultural card or official certificate to obtain fuel....

item.title: UIU, ULAB’s CSE awarded Washington Accord accreditation
item.description: The bachelor of science in computer science and engineering programme at the United International University and the University of Liberal Arts Bangladesh has recently been awarded the Washington Accord accreditation....

item.title: 43 dengue cases detected in 24hrs
item.description: The Directorate General of Health Services said that they recorded 43 fresh cases of dengue onslaughts in the past 24 hours ending at 8:00am on Monday....

item.title: RU Chhatra Dal leader assaults shopkeeper over price dispute
item.description: A leader of Jatiyatabadi Chhatra Dal, the student wing of the ruling Bangladesh Nationalist Party, allegedly assaulted a shopkeeper and his employee at Rajshahi University campus on Sunday night following a dispute over service charges....

item.title: War on, Iran rejects fresh US ultimatum
item.description: Iran has rejected a proposed truce in its war with the United States and Israel, state media reported Monday, despite the stark threat by US president Donald Trump to destroy its vital infrastructure....

item.title: DCCI flags slow pvt credit growth, high lending rates
item.description: The Dhaka Chamber of Commerce and Industry on Monday expressed concern over slowdown in private sector credit growth, which has declined to 6.03 per cent — the lowest in 22 years......Geographic Reference

item.title: Energy shock from Iran war dims Egypt economy
item.description: At a roadside cafe in downtown Cairo, Abu Ali was mid domino throw when the lights snapped off under new early-closing orders enacted to curb Egypt’s soaring energy bill due to the US-Israel war on Iran......

item.title: TIB slams govt for ‘backtracking’ on converting ordinances into laws
item.description: Transparency International Bangladesh on Monday castigated the government for its policy regarding the conversion of ordinances issued by the previous interim government into laws....

item.title: S Korea to send ships to Saudi Red Sea port
item.description: South Korea will send five Korean-flagged ships to the Saudi Arabian Red Sea port of Yanbu to help establish alternative oil supply routes to avoid the disruption in the Strait of Hormuz, a ruling MP said Monday......

item.title: Tension grips Patgram border as BSF shoots at Bangladesh youth
item.description: A tense situation gripped the bordering areas of Patgram in Lalmonirhat district after a Bangladeshi youth was injured in firing by India’s Border Security Force along the border on Monday morning....Human rights reports

item.title: Hospitals struggle as daily infections cross 1,000
item.description: The measles situation has worsened as the number of suspected measles patients crossed 1,000 in a single day and hospitals across Bangladesh struggle to cope with the growing influx of infected children....
